Title: A soybean EF-Tu family protein GmEF8, an interactor of GmCBL1, enhances drought and heat tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis and soybean

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: GmEF8 encodes a 49.28-kDa protein closely related to EF-Tu cytosolic counterpart EF1-alpha proteins from other species and is orthologous to Arabidopsis AT5G60390. GmEF8 expression rescues the drought- and heat-sensitive phenotype of the Arabidopsis EF-Tu knockout mutant.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmEF8 is a soybean EF-Tu family protein that physically interacts with the Ca2+ sensor GmCBL1, as shown by yeast two-hybrid, BiFC, and GST pull-down assays. 2. GmEF8 localizes mainly to the cytoplasm and nucleus and its transcript is rapidly induced by drought, heat, and ABA but not strongly by salt. 3. GmEF8 expression rescues the drought- and heat-sensitive phenotype of an Arabidopsis EF-Tu T-DNA knockout mutant. 4. Overexpression of GmEF8 in Arabidopsis enhances drought and heat tolerance, increasing root growth and survival under stress. 5. Overexpression of GmEF8 in soybean hairy roots improves drought and heat tolerance, with higher RWC and proline and lower MDA, whereas RNAi knockdown causes hypersensitivity. 6. GmEF8 overexpression upregulates drought-responsive genes GmP5CS and GmRD22, indicating a positive regulatory role in abiotic stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GmEF8 positively regulates abiotic stress tolerance. Overexpression in Arabidopsis and soybean hairy roots increases drought and heat tolerance, with longer roots, greater root surface area, higher survival under drought, higher RWC and proline, and lower MDA, whereas RNAi knockdown in soybean hairy roots causes hypersensitivity with severe growth inhibition and leaf wilting under stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: GmEF8 physically interacts with the Ca2+ sensor GmCBL1 at the plasma membrane and localizes to the cytoplasm and nucleus. Overexpression of GmEF8 leads to upregulation of drought-responsive genes GmP5CS and GmRD22 and enhanced osmotic adjustment (increased proline, higher RWC, reduced MDA) under drought and heat stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Acts in a Ca2+-sensor (GmCBL1)-associated abiotic stress signaling pathway that positively regulates drought and heat stress responses in soybean.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: GmEF8 -->(activation)--> GmP5CS (drought-responsive gene), evidenced by higher GmP5CS expression in OE-GmEF8 compared with EV and MR-GmEF8 hairy roots under drought. GmEF8 -->(activation)--> GmRD22, with increased GmRD22 transcript levels in OE-GmEF8 compared to EV and MR-GmEF8 plants.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Elongation factor Tu (EF-Tu) family protein, cytosolic counterpart EF1-alpha-like GTPase.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasm and nucleus, based on GmEF8-GFP fusion localization in Arabidopsis leaf mesophyll protoplasts.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GmCBL1 (soybean calcineurin B-like 1), validated by yeast two-hybrid, BiFC in Arabidopsis mesophyll protoplasts, and GST pull-down assays.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: GmEF8 transcript levels are rapidly and strongly induced by drought, heat, and ABA treatments in soybean seedlings, peaking around 1–2 h, whereas expression changes little in response to salt treatment.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress simulated with PEG6000; heat stress (Arabidopsis at 45 °C, soybean at 42 °C); ABA spray (100 μM); salt stress (200 mmol/L NaCl) used for expression analysis.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought and heat) and Ca2+-sensor-mediated signaling in soybean.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance, specifically drought tolerance and heat tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In Arabidopsis, GmEF8-overexpression lines show significantly greater root length and root surface area than WT under PEG-induced drought and heat stress, and drought survival rates of approximately 85% compared to 45% for WT. In soybean hairy roots, OE-GmEF8 plants display higher RWC and proline content and lower MDA than EV and MR-GmEF8 lines under drought and heat stress.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Under non-stress conditions, GmEF8 overexpression lines in Arabidopsis and soybean hairy roots show growth similar to controls, indicating that GmEF8 mainly affects stress responses without major growth penalties.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Soybean cultivar Williams 82 composite plants with transgenic hairy roots (OE-GmEF8, MR-GmEF8, EV); Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Col-0 wild-type plants; Arabidopsis T-DNA insertion mutant SALK_035228C (ortholog of GmEF8); pCAMBIA3301-based overexpression and RNAi vectors; pBI121-based 35S::GmEF8 construct; Arabidopsis mesophyll protoplasts for localization and interaction assays.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: qRT-PCR to quantify GmEF8 and stress-responsive gene expression; PEG6000-induced drought and high-temperature treatments in soybean seedlings and Arabidopsis; generation of Arabidopsis 35S::GmEF8 overexpression lines and complementation lines; soybean hairy root transformation for GmEF8 overexpression and RNAi knockdown; measurement of root length, root surface area, plant survival, RWC, proline, and MDA; yeast two-hybrid, BiFC, and GST pull-down assays for protein interaction; GFP-based subcellular localization in Arabidopsis protoplasts.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 35S-driven overexpression of GmEF8 in Arabidopsis; complementation of an Arabidopsis EF-Tu T-DNA mutant with GmEF8; overexpression and RNAi-mediated knockdown of GmEF8 in soybean hairy root composite plants; phenotypic and physiological assays under PEG-induced drought and heat stress; yeast two-hybrid, BiFC, and GST pull-down to validate interaction with GmCBL1; GFP fusion subcellular localization in Arabidopsis protoplasts.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a GmCBL1-interacting protein by yeast two-hybrid screening of a soybean seedling cDNA library, followed by cloning of the full-length GmEF8 coding sequence for functional analyses.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Overexpression of GmEF8 in soybean hairy root composite plants enhances drought and heat tolerance by increasing relative water content and proline levels and reducing MDA accumulation; GmEF8 also induces drought-responsive genes (e.g., GmRD22, GmP5CS). These results support using GmEF8 as a candidate gene for engineering or breeding soybean cultivars with improved drought and heat tolerance.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: AT5G60390 is an EF-Tu cytosolic counterpart EF1-alpha-like protein and the closest Arabidopsis homolog of soybean GmEF8. Its mutant phenotype is used as a background to test complementation by GmEF8, and GmEF8 expression partially rescues the drought- and heat-sensitive phenotype.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AT5G60390 is the closest Arabidopsis homolog of soybean GmEF8, an EF-Tu cytosolic counterpart EF1-alpha-like protein. 2. A T-DNA insertion mutant (SALK_035228C) lacking functional AT5G60390 displays normal growth under control conditions but shows increased sensitivity to drought and heat stress, with reduced root length and surface area compared to WT. 3. Overexpression of soybean GmEF8 in this mutant partially rescues the drought- and heat-sensitive phenotype, supporting a positive role of AT5G60390 in abiotic stress tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of AT5G60390 function in the T-DNA insertion mutant (SALK_035228C) causes increased sensitivity to drought and heat stress, with reduced root length and root surface area under stress, while growth is similar to WT under normal conditions, indicating that AT5G60390 positively regulates drought and heat tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress simulated by growing seedlings on MS medium containing 6% PEG6000 and soil-grown plants subjected to water withholding; heat stress imposed by incubating seedlings at 45 °C for 2 h followed by recovery.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Molecular basis of EF-Tu-like factor in abiotic stress (drought and heat) tolerance in Arabidopsis.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance, specifically drought tolerance and heat tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under PEG-induced drought and heat stress, the AT5G60390 mutant exhibits significantly reduced total root length and root surface area compared with WT plants, while values are similar under control conditions.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Under normal growth conditions, the AT5G60390 T-DNA mutant shows a phenotype similar to WT plants, indicating that the major observed effect of AT5G60390 loss is on stress responses rather than baseline growth.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Col-0 wild-type plants and a T-DNA insertion mutant line SALK_035228C disrupting AT5G60390; seedlings grown on MS medium and in soil for drought and heat stress assays.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: PCR screening to identify a homozygous T-DNA insertion mutant of AT5G60390 (SALK_035228C); PEG6000-containing MS medium and high-temperature treatments to impose drought and heat stress; measurement of total root length and root surface area to quantify stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Analysis of a homozygous T-DNA insertion knockout mutant SALK_035228C (AT5G60390) under PEG-induced drought and heat stress, comparing root length and root surface area to WT Col-0 plants.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified from the Arabidopsis protein database as the closest homolog to soybean GmEF8 (96.44% identity) and selected for functional analysis using a T-DNA insertion mutant.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: AT5G60390 encodes an EF-Tu/EF1-alpha-like protein that positively regulates drought and heat tolerance in Arabidopsis; its loss-of-function mutant shows hypersensitivity to these stresses. Functional characterization of AT5G60390 provides a model for EF-Tu-mediated abiotic stress tolerance and supports targeting its crop homologs, such as soybean GmEF8, for improving drought and heat tolerance in crops.
